channel.title: The Grant Desk - Tech & Infrastructure, EU
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Tech & Infrastructure, EU.

item.title: SIDN fonds: Pioneer Projects for a Strong Internet (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: SIDN fonds

Location: Netherlands. Open to natural persons and legal entities for the pioneer track; theme calls require legal entities.

Award: Up to EUR 10,000 per pioneer project; theme calls (once or twice a year) fund larger projects above EUR 10,000. No application fee.

Duration: Pioneer projects develop an idea into a demo, pilot or experimental design; continuous intake.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

SIDN fonds (the foundation behind the .nl domain registry) invests in daring projects with societal added value that contribute to a strong internet and strong internet users. The Pioneers track is continuously open and awards up to EUR 10,000 to develop an innovative idea into a demo, pilot or experimental design; it is open to both individuals and legal entities. Larger projects are funded through theme calls issued once or twice a year (legal entities only). Projects must show innovation and public value and share their results openly; plain website or app building and purely B2B proposals are excluded. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: WBSO: R&D Tax Credit (rolling, Netherlands)
item.description:
Organisation: Rijksdienst voor Ondernemend Nederland (RVO)

Location: Netherlands. Dutch companies and self-employed paying payroll or income tax that conduct qualifying R&D.

Award: Payroll/income tax reduction for R&D, not a cash grant. 2026: 36 percent of the first EUR 380,000 of R&D wage costs (50 percent for startups), 16 percent above; fixed deduction for self-employed. National budget EUR 1.817 billion for 2026. No application fee.

Duration: Applied for in advance per period; cannot start before approval.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The WBSO (Wet Bevordering Speur- en Ontwikkelingswerk) is the Dutch R&D tax credit administered by RVO. It reduces payroll/wage tax for companies with R&D staff and provides an income-tax deduction for self-employed people doing R&D; it is a tax credit, not a cash grant. For 2026 the benefit is 36 percent of the first EUR 380,000 of R&D wage and other costs (50 percent for qualifying startups) and 16 percent above that; self-employed receive a fixed deduction (and need at least 500 R&D hours per year). Eligible applicants are Dutch companies and self-employed people paying payroll or income tax who carry out qualifying R&D in the EU. Applications are made in advance via Mijn RVO and cannot be retroactive; for coverage of the final three months of a year the deadline is 30 September. Time-keeping and project administration are required. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: UNIIQ: Proof-of-Concept Fund (rolling, Zuid-Holland)
item.description:
Organisation: UNIIQ

Location: Zuid-Holland (South Holland), Netherlands only. Early-stage research-driven startups.

Award: Around EUR 300,000 per company as a convertible loan (investment, not a grant). Total fund EUR 50 million. No application fee.

Duration: Investment via convertible loan; rolling intake.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

UNIIQ is a proof-of-concept investment fund for the earliest, riskiest phase of company building in Zuid-Holland (South Holland). It invests around EUR 300,000 per company as a convertible loan (for example redeemed over about three years or converted at a follow-on valuation), drawn from a EUR 50 million fund. It is an investment, not a grant. Eligible companies are early-stage, research-driven startups based in Zuid-Holland, typically in life sciences, deep tech and high tech. Intake is continuous via an orientation meeting followed by a funding application. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ROM InWest: Startup and Scale-up Financing (rolling, Noord-Holland)
item.description:
Organisation: ROM InWest (Regional Development Company for Noord-Holland)

Location: Noord-Holland (North Holland), Netherlands. Startups, scale-ups and innovative SMEs.

Award: Financing of EUR 300,000 to EUR 5,000,000 as loans, equity participations or a combination (not a gift); cannot exceed 49.9 percent of a financing round. This is investment, not a grant. No application fee.

Duration: Investment; rolling intake.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

ROM InWest is the regional development company for Noord-Holland (North Holland). It finances startups, scale-ups and innovative SMEs working on food, energy, healthcare and circular-economy transitions, providing EUR 300,000 to EUR 5,000,000 as loans, equity participations or a combination (explicitly not a gift), and not exceeding 49.9 percent of a financing round. It is an investment, not a grant. Companies must be based in Noord-Holland. Intake is continuous, starting with a quick-scan tool. No application fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eurostars Call 11 2026 (Eureka / Eurostars-3)
item.description:
Organisation: Eureka / Eurostars-3 (Dutch participation via RVO)

Location: International consortia from at least two Eurostars countries, led by an innovative SME. Dutch funding administered by RVO.

Award: Co-funds collaborative international R&D; national funding varies by country. For the Dutch project share: up to EUR 500,000 per project at 50 percent reimbursement of eligible R&D costs. No application fee.

Duration: Projects up to 36 months.

Deadline: 10 Sep 2026

Eurostars (part of the Eureka network, the Eurostars-3 programme co-funded by Horizon Europe) supports collaborative international R&D projects led by innovative SMEs. Each country funds its own participants, so amounts vary; for the Dutch share, RVO funds up to EUR 500,000 per project at a 50 percent reimbursement rate on eligible R&D costs. Projects require an international consortium of at least two independent organisations from at least two Eurostars countries, led by an innovative SME, with the SME budget at least 50 percent of the total and no single participant exceeding 70 percent. Projects must run no longer than 36 months and be civil (non-defence) in purpose. Call 11 opens 9 July 2026 and closes 10 September 2026, 14:00 CEST. No application fee. Submit via the Eureka project platform (myeurekaproject.org); Dutch national information at RVO.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: MIT R&D Collaboration Projects 2026 (MKB Innovatiestimulering Topsectoren)
item.description:
Organisation: Rijksdienst voor Ondernemend Nederland (RVO)

Location: Netherlands. SME consortia (minimum two collaborating SMEs).

Award: Grant covering 35 percent of eligible costs. Small projects EUR 50,000-200,000; large projects EUR 200,000-350,000 per project (EUR 25,000-100,000 or EUR 25,000-175,000 per participant). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; ranked tender (not first-come).

Deadline: 15 Sep 2026

The MIT R&D Collaboration Projects scheme (MIT R&D-samenwerkingsprojecten, part of MKB Innovatiestimulering Topsectoren) funds joint R&D projects by Dutch SMEs within the national Top Sectors and Mission-driven Innovation policy. The grant covers 35 percent of eligible costs: small projects of EUR 50,000-200,000 and large projects of EUR 200,000-350,000 (with per-participant ranges of EUR 25,000-100,000 for small and EUR 25,000-175,000 for large). Eligible applicants are consortia of at least two collaborating SMEs, with no single participant carrying more than 70 percent of costs. The national RVO round runs 9 June to 15 September 2026 (closes 17:00) and is a ranked tender, not first-come-first-served; some provinces run separate regional MIT windows. No application fee. Apply via RVO.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Digital Culture Grant Scheme 2026 (final round)
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands. Requires Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KvK) registration.

Award: EUR 10,000-25,000 for solo projects; EUR 10,000-50,000 for collaborative projects with partners. At least 20 percent of total project costs must be covered by co-financing. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based.

Deadline: 19 Aug 2026

The Stimuleringsfonds Digital Culture Grant Scheme (Deelregeling Digitale cultuur) supports projects by makers, researchers, design studios and cultural institutions positioned professionally in digital culture. Solo projects can receive EUR 10,000-25,000; collaborative projects with partners can receive up to EUR 50,000. At least 20 percent of total project costs must be covered by co-financing. Applicants must be registered with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KvK). The 2026 application window for this round runs 12-19 August 2026 (opens 12 August 15:00, closes 19 August 16:00 CEST); in 2026 the scheme runs only two rounds and this is the last. No application fee. Apply via the official scheme page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: EIC Accelerator 2026 (European Innovation Council)
item.description:
Organisation: European Innovation Council (European Commission)

Location: EU Member States and Horizon Europe Associated Countries. Single-company applicants; third-country applicants must relocate before the full-application stage.

Award: Grant up to EUR 2.5 million (non-dilutive lump sum) for innovation activities, plus optional equity investment of EUR 1-10 million via the EIC Fund. Options: grant-only, blended finance, or investment-only. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; supports development and scale-up at TRL 5/6 to 8.

Deadline: Closed 08 Jul 2026

The EIC Accelerator funds high-risk, high-impact deep-tech SMEs and start-ups to develop and scale breakthrough innovations. It offers a grant of up to EUR 2.5 million (a non-dilutive lump sum) for innovation activities, and can combine this with an equity investment of EUR 1-10 million through the EIC Fund (blended finance), or provide investment only. The Step 1 short application can be submitted at any time; full (Step 2) applications are evaluated at fixed cut-offs. The next 2026 cut-offs are 8 July, 2 September and 4 November 2026 (all 17:00 Brussels time). Eligible applicants are for-profit SMEs and start-ups (fewer than 250 employees, turnover up to EUR 50M or balance sheet up to EUR 43M) established in an EU Member State or Horizon Europe Associated Country; small mid-caps up to 499 employees are eligible for investment-only support. Apply via the EU Funding and Tenders portal (topic HORIZON-EIC-2026-ACCELERATOR-01).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prototype Fund Class 03 (Open-Source Software)
item.description:
Organisation: Prototype Fund (Open Knowledge Foundation Germany)

Location: Germany / EU (lead must be based in Germany; funded members must reside in the EU)

Award: Up to EUR 95,000 over 6 months, or up to EUR 158,000 over 10 months, plus coaching. No application fee.

Duration: 6 or 10 months

Deadline: 30 Nov 2026

Prototype Fund supports the development of socially relevant open-source software, offering low-threshold funding and coaching to individual developers and small teams. ELIGIBILITY: individual developers or teams of up to four; funded members must reside in the EU and the lead entity must be based in Germany. Application window 1 October to 30 November 2026. Apply via the Prototype Fund application page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Council of Europe: New Democratic Pact for Europe Democracy Hackathon 2026 (Strasbourg)
item.description:
Organisation: Council of Europe (under the Monaco Presidency of the Committee of Ministers), in partnership with No Hate Speech Week and Microsoft

Location: Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, France (hybrid: four online warm-up sessions leading to a three-day in-person finale)

Award: EUR 50,000 Microsoft-funded prize pool distributed among winning teams. Travel and accommodation covered for the 20 selected finalist teams. No application fee.

Duration: Online warm-up sessions plus three-day in-person hackathon (17-19 June 2026)

Deadline: Closed 05 Jun 2026

Council of Europe civic-tech hackathon to combat online hate speech and develop tech solutions for democracy, run alongside No Hate Speech Week and backed by a Microsoft prize pool. Four challenge tracks cover privacy-preserving hate speech detection, identifying coordinated inauthentic behaviour, gender-based violence reporting systems, and innovative social transformation approaches. ELIGIBILITY: pre-formed multidisciplinary teams of 3-4 (typically a technologist/developer, a democracy or policy expert, and a designer or UX innovator); residents of the 46 Council of Europe member states; coding experience is not required for every team member. FORMAT: four online warm-up sessions, then a three-day in-person finale at the Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, 17-19 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mozilla Festival (MozFest) 2026: Call for Proposals - Wilding
item.description:
Organisation: Mozilla Foundation

Location: Barcelona, Spain (Recinte Fabra i Coats; co-hosted with the City of Barcelona as part of Open Tech Week)

Award: Limited travel support, not guaranteed: flights to Barcelona booked through Mozilla's platform; a local hotel room reserved by Mozilla; a 5-day Metro pass including airport transfer. No additional funds for food, other ground transport or travel insurance. Visa fee reimbursements handled through a separate process. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Three days, 28-30 October 2026

Deadline: Closed 24 May 2026

MozFest 2026 is Mozilla Foundation's annual festival of debates, labs and workshops, forums, showcases, talks and installations - this year at Recinte Fabra i Coats in Barcelona (28-30 October 2026), co-hosted with the City of Barcelona during Open Tech Week. 2026 THEME 'WILDING': a provocation about loosening rigid systems and creating conditions for communities and technologies to evolve collectively, naturally and intuitively. Inspired by ecological rewilding, it explores community-owned mesh networks, federated platforms, open protocols, community archives, and tools built on transparency, trust and mutual flourishing. Mozilla is inviting proposals that exemplify Wilding and celebrate community-led alternatives - work that is proof of what is possible. FESTIVAL TRACKS: Wilding Advocacy; Wilding Knowledge; Wilding Systems; Wilding With Nature; Youth Wilding; Wilding Creativity; Founders Wilding; Developers Wilding. PROCESS: submit a proposal via the MozFest CFP on the Mozilla Foundation festival site; travel support requests are reviewed after the CFP closes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NGI Fediversity (11th call)
item.description:
Organisation: NLnet / NGI Fediversity (funded by EU Horizon Europe)

Location: Worldwide

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Closed 01 Jun 2026

Small to medium-sized R&D grants for projects making federated and self-hosted internet services easier to deploy and operate at scale. Funds practical work on portable Fediverse-style infrastructure, hosted Mastodon-like or PeerTube-like services, secure email, private cloud and other tools that support decentralisation and digital self-determination. Open to individuals and organisations of any type; results released under free or open-source licences.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NGI TALER (13th call): Privacy-Preserving Digital Payments
item.description:
Organisation: NLnet / NGI TALER (with GNU Taler community, funded by EU Horizon Europe and Swiss SERI)

Location: Worldwide

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project (€676,000 total budget for open calls)

Duration: Project-based (programme runs to November 2026)

Deadline: Closed 01 Jun 2026

Small to medium-sized R&D grants for free and open source efforts aligned with privacy-preserving digital payments. Build new GNU Taler capabilities, auxiliary tools, UX, FOSS-app and open-standard integrations (P2P micropayments in messengers, social media, video conferencing), or merchant-backend improvements. All results released under a free or open-source licence. Strong projects can scale up via NGI0 Core.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NGI Zero Commons Fund (13th call)
item.description:
Organisation: NLnet / NGI Zero (funded by EU Horizon Europe and Swiss SERI)

Location: Worldwide

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project (scalable for proven potential)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Closed 01 Jun 2026

Small to medium-sized R&D grants to help reclaim the public nature of the internet across the full technology spectrum: libre silicon, middleware, P2P infrastructure, end-user applications, open standards, open data and AI, open science, creative commons and open educational resources. Project results released under free or open-source licences. Aimed at troubleshooters tackling hard, important internet-commons challenges. Total programme: €21.6m through 2027.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
